# Code of Conduct

We want this project to be a kind, productive place to work. The rules are simple.

## Be respectful

Disagree with ideas, not people. Critique code, not authors. Assume good intent — if a comment reads harshly, ask before reacting.

## Be specific

Vague criticism (“this is bad”) helps no one. Point at the line, propose the fix, or ask the question.

## Be honest

If you don’t know something, say so. If you broke something, say so. If a rule no longer serves the project, open an ADR proposing a change — don’t quietly ignore it.

## Be inclusive

No discrimination, harassment, or personal attacks of any kind. People bring different backgrounds, experience levels, and communication styles. Make room for all of them.

## Reporting

If something here is being violated, contact the project maintainer directly. Reports are confidential.

## Scope

This applies to all project spaces — issues, pull requests, discussions, chat — and to public spaces when an individual is representing the project.
